mission.title: knative: Readiness probe fails if the container restarts after a liveness probe fail
mission.description: Readiness probe fails if the container restarts after a liveness probe fail. Community-reported issue.

steps.title: Identify knative troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your knative deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n knative -l app.kubernetes.io/name=knative
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=knative -n knative --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review knative configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant knative configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n knative -l app.kubernetes.io/name=knative
kubectl get configmap -n knative -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=knative
```
## What version of Knative?
v1.2

## Expected Behavior
After a liveness probe fail, the container should restart and ideally should start serving the traffic again, just like how it would happen for a k8s deployment.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Readiness probe fails if the container restarts after a…
steps.description:
Thanks for the deep breakdown everyone -  I think what we really need is a clear signal to distinguish between the Pod shutting down and either container (queue-proxy/user-container) restarting. 

I'm not sure that's straightforward. Will dig into this for the next release.

I worry about the
```yaml
apiVersion: serving.knative.dev/v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: healthprobes-go
spec:
  template:
    spec:
      containers:
        - image: shashankft/healthprobes@sha256:cc1cb4323e4d1cdee62e3ae7623ff6a213db72e89c17ad4772c39fe841b98fb9
          env:
            - name: TARGET
              value: "Go Sample v1"
          livenessProbe:
            httpGet:
              path: /healthz/liveness
              port: 0
            periodSeconds: 15
            failureThreshould: 1
```

steps.title: Confirm Readiness probe fails if the container restarts… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=knative -n knative --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n knative --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
Thanks for the deep breakdown everyone -  I think what we really need is a clear signal to distinguish between the Pod shutting down and either container (queue-proxy/user-container) restarting. 

I'm not sure that's straightforward. Will dig into this for the next release.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with knative installed or the issue environment reproducible.
